title: Bridge BLE packet transport to LCI ResourceTransport
description: The Python TUI can use LciClient over IP/CoAP ResourceTransport, but BlePacketTransport is currently only a packet transport. Implement a CoAP-over-packet ResourceTransport adapter (or equivalent bridge) so TUI messaging/status/config flows can run over BLE without UI-specific branching.
acceptance_criteria: LciClient can use a BLE-backed ResourceTransport; request/observe paths are tested with fake BLE packet transport; TUI messaging flow works with the same client interface for BLE and IP.
notes:
Implemented no-hardware BLE packet-to-LCI ResourceTransport bridge. Added python/src/lichen/client/packet_coap.py with PacketCoapConfig, PacketCoapResourceTransport, PacketCoapResourceSubscription, PacketSendScope, and PacketDatagramChannel. The adapter frames aiocoap CoAP datagrams as IPv6/UDP packets over any PacketTransport, including BlePacketTransport, validates inbound IPv6 destination/UDP ports/checksum before dispatch, uses operation-local send scopes so request/initial Observe write failures are attributed to the correct operation, and closes the packet CoAP session on send failure so later calls fail fast until reconnect. Exported the adapter from lichen.client. Wired native TUI build_messaging_client/main with --ble-address, --ble-local-host, --ble-node-host and LinkMode.BLE while preserving the IP/CoAP path. Added tests/client/test_packet_coap.py for GET status, send_message, Observe inbox, close behavior, packet send failure, Observe send failure, inner subscription cleanup, inbound packet filtering, and valid local CoAP dispatch. Extended tests/tui/test_native.py for BLE client construction, CLI wiring, and same LciClient UI flow in BLE mode.
Commands run: cd python && uv run pytest tests/client/test_packet_coap.py -q => 11 passed; cd python && uv run pytest tests/tui/test_native.py -q => 37 passed before later packet test additions; cd python && uv run pytest tests/client/test_packet_coap.py tests/tui/test_native.py -q => 42 passed earlier, then packet tests expanded; cd python && uv run pytest tests/client tests/tui/test_native.py -q => 89 passed; cd python && uv run pytest tests/test_packaging.py tests/client/test_packet_coap.py tests/client/test_lci.py tests/tui/test_native.py -q => 59 passed; cd python && uv run pytest tests/client tests/tui/test_native.py tests/coap/test_messages_resource.py tests/coap/test_transport.py -q => final 115 passed in 10.47s; cd python && uv run ruff check src/lichen/client src/lichen/tui/native.py tests/client tests/tui/test_native.py => All checks passed; cd python && uv run mypy src/lichen/client/packet_coap.py => Success: no issues found in 1 source file; git diff --check => passed. No EC2 used because work was Python/no-hardware only.
Review evidence: three independent code review agents reviewed the generated code/tests. Initial findings fixed before closure: P2 inbound packet dispatch accepted any UDP packet; fixed with IPv6 dst, UDP next-header, source/destination port, nonzero checksum, and checksum validation plus drop tests. P2 send failures were detached from request lifecycle; fixed with operation-local PacketSendScope and immediate CoapTransportError. P2 Observe initial send failure could escape/stale-poison later operations; fixed by carrying the same send scope from observe() creation through the first result. P2 Observe send-failure path did not close the inner subscription; fixed and tested. P2 send failure could wedge aiocoap session; fixed by closing the packet CoAP transport on send failure and asserting later calls fail fast as not connected. Final review confirmations from Einstein and Dewey reported no P0/P1/P2 findings; Boyle also cleared the per-operation scope before the final typed-helper change. Reviewers repeatedly noted unrelated untracked lichen/boards/renode/nucleo_wl55jc/_integration_test.resc has absolute paths; it remains untracked and outside this bead/commit.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware scope: LciClient can now use a BLE-backed PacketCoapResourceTransport over BlePacketTransport/PacketTransport, TUI CLI can select BLE mode without UI-specific client branching, request/send/observe paths are covered with fake packet transports, and review/test evidence is recorded.
